fingerprint: Entire Schumann suppression claim built on globe-cavity frequency; dome's own H₀ gives wrong baseline; 30% threshold has no dome derivation
globe_value: f₁ = c·√2/(2πR) ≈ 10.59 Hz (raw) → 7.83 Hz (with ionospheric losses measured on globe)
dome_value: f = c/(4H) with H₀ = 8537 km → predicts 8.78 Hz, not 7.83 Hz; dome uses H = 9572 km reverse-engineered from the globe observation
match_precision: H₀ = 8537 km gives 8.78 Hz — 12% error from the 7.83 Hz WIN-061 depends on. The 'correct' H = 9572 km is reverse-engineered from the globe measurement.

analysis.wrong_formula_type: The dome uses f = c/(4H), a 1D quarter-wave resonator formula. Schumann resonances are spherical standing waves described by f_n = c·√(n(n+1))/(2πa) where a = R_Earth. These are mathematically different: quarter-wave resonance depends on cavity height, Schumann resonance depends on cavity circumference (2πR). The dome's formula happens to produce 7.83 Hz ONLY when H is fitted to the observed globe frequency — not derived from dome geometry.
analysis.h0_discrepancy: The dome's own cavity height H₀ = 8537 km (inject_ai_layer.py) gives f = c/(4×8537) = 8.78 Hz, not 7.83 Hz. WIN-029 claims H = c/(4×7.83) = 9572 km to match the observation, but this is a DIFFERENT height than H₀. The two dome parameters (H₀ = 8537 km from the exponential cavity profile, H = 9572 km from the Schumann reverse-derivation) disagree by 1,035 km (10.8%). This internal inconsistency means the dome cannot self-consistently produce 7.83 Hz.
analysis.suppression_not_derived: The 30% amplitude drop threshold (monitor.py Domains #16 and #30) is a bare assertion. No formula in the dome repository connects dome geometry, aetheric medium properties, or cavity parameters to amplitude suppression magnitude during geomagnetic storms. Standard ionospheric physics derives this from measured D-region conductivity changes: storm-time particle precipitation increases σ by factor ~5, altering the cavity Q-factor from ~6 to ~4, producing ~30-40% amplitude reduction. The dome adopts the number without derivation.
analysis.copper_contradicts_suppression: WIN-034 claims the firmament is cast copper/bronze. Copper conductivity (~6×10⁷ S/m) is constant and unaffected by geomagnetic storms. Schumann suppression requires a LOSSY boundary whose conductivity changes with particle precipitation — i.e., an ionosphere, not a metal shell. The dome's own firmament material predicts storms should NOT suppress Schumann resonance.
analysis.monitoring_disabled: Domains #16 (Kp≥5) and #30 (Kp≥7) in monitor.py have pass=None — scoring is disabled. WIN-061 is listed as 'CONFIRMED' while the dome's own monitoring infrastructure has never validated the claim. The code structure exists as a placeholder, not an active test.

code_search.found_in: monitor.py Domains #16 and #30 (Schumann Amplitude Suppression) — both have pass=None (disabled). inject_ai_layer.py: H0=8537 (cavity height parameter). No derivation of 30% threshold.
code_search.notes: 30% threshold is hardcoded assertion. No formula connects dome cavity geometry to suppression magnitude. H₀=8537 gives f=8.78 Hz, not the 7.83 Hz baseline that WIN-061's storm-suppression claim depends on.

globe_fingerprints_found.description: 1D quarter-wave formula f=c/(4H) substituted for spherical harmonic formula f=c·√(n(n+1))/(2πa). The Schumann resonance is a circumferential standing wave on a sphere; the dome treats it as a radial resonance in a cavity. The only reason f=c/(4H) can match 7.83 Hz is that H is fitted to the observed globe-derived frequency.

H = 9572 km (WIN-029) is computed as c/(4×7.83), using the globe's observed Schumann frequency as input. The dome's own H₀ = 8537 km gives 8.78 Hz instead. The 'correct' cavity height was derived FROM the globe observation, not from dome geometry.

The 7.83 Hz frequency itself encodes the Earth's radius: f = c·√2/(2πR) with loss correction gives 7.83 Hz for R ≈ 6371 km. Every dome claim about Schumann resonance (WIN-002, 029, 038, 061) inherits this globe fingerprint because the frequency is measured from the globe-shaped cavity.

why_it_matters: WIN-061 claims to explain Schumann storm-time suppression, but the entire Schumann resonance framework it depends on is globe-derived. The dome cannot self-consistently produce the 7.83 Hz baseline (H₀ gives 8.78 Hz), provides no mechanism or formula for suppression magnitude, and its proposed copper firmament would be immune to the conductivity changes that cause the suppression. This WIN adds no predictive content — it asserts that a globe-measured phenomenon 'matches' the dome model without deriving any dome-specific prediction.
suggested_text: The dome's Schumann cavity height H₀ = 8,537 km (from inject_ai_layer.py) predicts a baseline frequency of c/(4×8,537) = 8.78 Hz — not 7.83 Hz. The 'matching' height of 9,572 km (WIN-029) was reverse-engineered from the observed globe-cavity frequency, not derived from dome geometry. The dome uses a 1D quarter-wave formula (f = c/4H) to approximate a phenomenon that is actually a spherical standing wave (f = c√2/2πR). The formula only works when H is tuned to the globe's measured frequency. Meanwhile, the 30% amplitude suppression threshold in monitor.py has no dome derivation — it is borrowed from ionospheric physics of a spherical Earth-ionosphere cavity.
